blockers: Adapter must validate that Qwen CLI 0.19.2 output is a JSON array before parsing individual entries — missing isArray check could throw on malformed scalar or object output
Missing total_cost_usd fallback to budget_result=not_reported needs a unit test covering the null/undefined/missing key cases explicitly
is_error detail surfacing must include a max-length truncation guard to prevent oversized error payloads from bloating the review response
bugs_found: JSON.parse on CLI output lacks try/catch — a malformed response from Qwen CLI 0.19.2 will crash the adapter instead of returning a graceful error envelope
Schema validation error messages are passed through verbatim from the review schema validator — should be mapped to a stable, human-readable format for downstream consumers
The adapter does not enforce a timeout on the Qwen CLI subprocess invocation, which could hang indefinitely if the CLI process stalls
missing_tests: Test for empty JSON array [] input — should return a valid empty review result, not an error
Test for JSON array with mixed valid/invalid entries — adapter should surface partial results with per-entry error flags
Test for total_cost_usd = 0 (zero, not missing) — must distinguish between 'explicitly zero' and 'not reported'
Test for is_error = true with a very long error message (>4KB) — verify truncation behavior
Test for CLI subprocess exit code != 0 combined with partial stdout — verify error precedence and partial data handling
Property-based test: generate random JSON structures and assert the adapter either parses them into the review schema or returns a structured error
security_concerns: CLI output is parsed without sanitization — if the Qwen CLI output contains user-controlled data, it could inject into downstream structured output fields; apply allowlist validation on all parsed keys before forwarding
No rate-limiting or quota guard on the adapter itself — a caller could invoke it in a tight loop, each invocation spawning a Qwen CLI subprocess, consuming CPU and token budget unchecked
suggestions: Add a zod or ajv runtime schema guard on the parsed CLI output before mapping to the review envelope — fail fast with a clear 'upstream schema mismatch' error
Introduce a structured log line at adapter entry and exit (with request_id correlation) so that debugging malformed CLI output is traceable without reproducing the exact input
Consider a caching layer keyed on the review input hash — repeated reviews of unchanged code should short-circuit instead of re-invoking the CLI
Add a dry-run mode that validates the CLI output format and schema without executing the full review pipeline — useful for CI gating on adapter version upgrades
Document the exact Qwen CLI 0.19.2 JSON array shape in a companion .md file so future maintainers can diff the contract when CLI versions change
